databaseName.description: Tenant database name (optional). If database name is set as well as the instance number, port is not required.

defaultSchema.description: Default schema to be used. Can be empty.

instance.description: SAP HANA tenant instance number (optional). If instance number is set, port is not required.

port.description: SAP HANA server port (optional if database name filled). Typically this will be 443 for SAP HANA Cloud. For on-prem/multi-tenanted instances, use the corresponding port number

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Needed for verifying self-signed TLS Certs

tlsDisabled.description: Enable TLS/SSL encryption for the connection to SAP HANA. Enabled by default. Disable only when your SAP HANA instance does not have TLS configured (plaintext connections).

examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: basic authentication, TLS enabled (tlsDisabled false), and a 30-second timeout. The user must still supply jsonData.server, a port (or tenant instance + database name), jsonData.username, and secureJsonData.password to get a working datasource.

basicAuthInstance.summary: Basic auth over a tenant instance (on-prem / multi-tenant)
basicAuthInstance.description: Connect to a tenant database by instance number + database name instead of an explicit port. The backend derives the port as 3<instance>13 (e.g. instance '00' -> 30013). instance is stored as a string.

basicAuthPort.summary: Basic auth over host + port (SAP HANA Cloud)
basicAuthPort.description: The typical SAP HANA Cloud setup: a server address, port 443, a username, and a password. TLS is enabled by default.

tlsClientAuth.description: Mutual TLS: jsonData.tlsAuth=true authenticates with an X.509 client certificate and key instead of a username/password. Both secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey are required.

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
<redacted>
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

tlsDisabled.summary: Basic auth over a plaintext (non-TLS) connection
tlsDisabled.description: jsonData.tlsDisabled=true turns TLS off entirely. Use only when the SAP HANA instance has no TLS configured. The editor clears the other TLS switches when TLS is disabled.

tlsSkipVerify.summary: Basic auth skipping TLS verification (development only)
tlsSkipVerify.description: jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true accepts any server certificate. Use only for development against a server whose certificate cannot be verified.

tlsWithCACert.summary: Basic auth verifying the server against a custom CA
tlsWithCACert.description: jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true verifies the SAP HANA server certificate against a custom / self-signed CA supplied in secureJsonData.tlsCACert, alongside basic authentication.
